quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 6, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Nomenclature Practice: Mixed Compound Types
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Naming and Formulas for Ionic Compounds, Covalent Compounds, Acids, Polyatomic Ions, Transition Metals, Stock System

questions.question: Vanadium (III) sulfide is represented as:
questions.explanation: Vanadium(III) sulfide is V₂S₃. Vanadium(III) has a +3 charge (V³⁺), and sulfide is S²⁻ with a -2 charge. To balance charges, two V³⁺ (+6) combine with three S²⁻ (-6).

questions.question: HIO₃ is represented as:
questions.explanation: HIO₃ is named iodic acid. It contains the iodate ion (IO₃⁻). The pattern for iodine oxyacids follows: IO⁻ (hypoiodite) → hypoiodous acid, IO₂⁻ (iodite) → iodous acid, IO₃⁻ (iodate) → iodic acid, IO₄⁻ (periodate) → periodic acid. Hydroiodic acid (B) would be HI (binary acid).

questions.question: Co₂(HPO₄)₃ is represented as:
questions.explanation: Co₂(HPO₄)₃ is named cobalt(III) monohydrogen phosphate. It contains the monohydrogen phosphate ion (HPO₄²⁻). The Roman numeral (III) indicates cobalt has a +3 charge.

questions.question: Hydroxide ion is represented as:
questions.explanation: The hydroxide ion is represented as OH⁻. It consists of one oxygen atom and one hydrogen atom with an overall 1- charge.

questions.question: Zn(ClO₄)₂ is represented as:
questions.explanation: Zn(ClO₄)₂ is named zinc perchlorate. It contains the perchlorate ion (ClO₄⁻). Perchlorate has one more oxygen than chlorate (ClO₃⁻).

questions.question: Li₂O is represented as:
questions.explanation: Li₂O is named lithium oxide. It is an ionic compound containing the oxide ion (O²⁻). Lithium (Group 1) forms only one type of ion (+1), so no Roman numeral is needed.

questions.question: Which is the correct formula for iron(II) phosphate?
questions.explanation: Iron(II) phosphate is Fe₃(PO₄)₂. Iron(II) has a +2 charge (Fe²⁺), and phosphate is PO₄³⁻ with a -3 charge. To balance charges, three Fe²⁺ (+6) combine with two PO₄³⁻ (-6). Fe(PO₄)₂ (C) would incorrectly imply iron has a +4 charge, and Fe(PO₃)₂ (D) would be iron(II) phosphite.

questions.question: The formula for calcium phosphate is:
questions.explanation: Calcium phosphate is Ca₃(PO₄)₂. Calcium (Ca²⁺) has a +2 charge, and phosphate (PO₄³⁻) has a -3 charge. To balance charges, three Ca²⁺ (+6) combine with two PO₄³⁻ (-6). Ca₃P₂ (D) is calcium phosphide, and Ca₃(PO₃)₂ (E) is calcium phosphite.

questions.question: Which of the following is the formula for hydroiodic acid?
questions.explanation: Hydroiodic acid is HI. It is a binary acid (hydrogen combined with a halogen). The prefix 'hydro-' and suffix '-ic' indicate a binary acid. HIO₄ (A) is periodic acid, HIO₃ (B) is iodic acid, and HIO (C) is hypoiodous acid — all are oxyacids containing oxygen.

questions.question: N₂ is represented as:
questions.explanation: N₂ is named nitrogen gas. It is a diatomic molecule of the element nitrogen. Nitrate (A) is NO₃⁻, nitride (B) is N³⁻, and nitrium (C) is not a standard term.

questions.question: O₂²⁻ is represented as:
questions.explanation: O₂²⁻ is named the peroxide ion. It consists of two oxygen atoms with an overall 2- charge. The dioxide ion (A) is not a standard term, and oxygen ion (B) typically refers to O²⁻ (oxide).

questions.question: Carbon diselenide is represented as:
questions.explanation: Carbon diselenide is CSe₂. The prefix 'di-' indicates two selenium atoms. It is a covalent compound analogous to carbon disulfide (CS₂).

questions.question: PbF₄ is represented as:
questions.explanation: PbF₄ is named lead(IV) fluoride. Fluorine has a -1 charge, so four fluorines give -4. Therefore, lead must have a +4 charge, indicated by (IV).

questions.question: Mg₃N₂ is represented as:
questions.explanation: Mg₃N₂ is named magnesium nitride. It contains the nitride ion (N³⁻). Magnesium (Group 2) forms only one type of ion (+2), so no Roman numeral is needed.

questions.question: Cr(H₂PO₄)₃ is represented as:
questions.explanation: Cr(H₂PO₄)₃ is named chromium(III) dihydrogen phosphate. It contains the dihydrogen phosphate ion (H₂PO₄⁻). The Roman numeral (III) indicates chromium has a +3 charge.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered a wide range of nomenclature skills! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review polyatomic ions and their charges.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between similar-sounding ion names (-ate, -ite, -ide).
scoreMessages.0: Please review naming rules for different types of compounds including ionic, covalent, and acids.
